rulings.Case Title: AEGIS ASSET MANAGEMENT LLC VS. CBRE CAPITAL MARKETS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment Or Summary Adjudication; And Joinder In All Supporting Documents Filed Therewith, Including Separate Statement Of Undisputed Material Facts, Declarations, And Compendium Of Evidence; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Lisa Hiraide; Exhibits
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 4, 2025 line 1. DEFENDANT CIM GROUP, L. P., 246 FIRST STREET, (SF) OWNER, LLC MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION; AND JOINDER IN ALL SUPPORTING DOCUMENTS FILED THEREWITH, INCLUDING SEPARATE STATEMENT OF UNDISPUTED MATERIAL FACTS, DECLARATIONS, AND COMPENDIUM OF EVIDENCE; MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES; DECLARATION OF LISA HIRAIDE; EXHIBITS is DENIED. There are triable issues of fact regarding whether Defendant made promises and false representations of fact regarding the bid date and specific bidding procedures. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: AEGIS ASSET MANAGEMENT LLC VS. CBRE CAPITAL MARKETS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Notice Of Motion
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 4, 2025 line 1 DEFENDANT CBRE, INC., ERRONEOUSLY SUED AS CBRE CAPITAL MARKETS, INC. AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND NOTICE OF MOTION is DENIED. There are triable issues of fact regarding whether Defendant made promises and false representations of fact regarding the bid date and specific bidding procedures. Plaintiff's request to strike the table in Defendant's Reply Separate Statement is GRANTED; the table's use of three columns violates Rule 3.1350(h). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MELISSA WILLIAMSON VS. BACO REALTY CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joint Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Order, Transfer Venue And For Monetary Sanctions And To Consolidate Non-Complex Actions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 4, 2025 line 4. DEFENDANT BACO REALTY CORPORATION AKA BACO PROPERTIES, BACO LLC, SECURITY PUBLIC STORAGE- RICHMOND LLC, MATT GIFFORD, CHANTEL INGALLS, RICHARD ESPINOZA, NICK MITCHELL, BENJAMIN EISLER, MICHAEL ORWITZ, MICHAEL EISLER JOINT NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO ENFORCE ORDER, TRANSFER VENUE AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS AND TO CONSOLIDATE NON-COMPLEX ACTIONS is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Transfer of venue to Alameda County is granted. Consolidation of actions is denied. This Court has no authority to consolidate this action with an action pending in another county. Sanctions are denied. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
